questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Here's correct calculation:N = A-Z = 235-92 = 143N = A-Z = 238-92 = 146
Number of electrons are same in both the uraniums that is 92.
Number of protons are same in both uraniums that is 92.
This is incorrect as atoms are given in the question.

questions.explanation: Uranium-235 and Uranium-238 are isotopes, meaning they have the same number of protons (92) but differ in the number of neutrons. Uranium-238 has 238 - 92 = 146 neutrons, while Uranium-235 has 235 - 92 = 143 neutrons, so it has 3 more neutrons.

questions.text: If 92U233 decays twice by α-emission, the resulting specie will be
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: When 92U233 decays twice by α-emission, it loses two alpha particles, which each consist of two protons and two neutrons. This results in the loss of four protons and four neutrons from the original nucleus. The resulting isotopes will be 88Ra225, which has 88 protons and 225 nucleons (protons + neutrons), and 86Rn221, which has 86 protons and 221 nucleons.

When 92U233 decays twice by α-emission, it loses two alpha particles, which each consist of two protons and two neutrons. This results in the loss of four protons and four neutrons from the original nucleus. questions.text: For skin cancer, which of the following can be used:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Iodine-131 is a radioactive isotope of iodine. It is primarily used for the treatment of thyroid cancer and other thyroid-related conditions. It is not typically used for the treatment of skin cancer because it does not provide the desired depth of penetration and is more suitable for thyroid-specific conditions.

Carbon-14 is a radioactive isotope of carbon. It is not commonly used in the treatment of skin cancer. Carbon-14 is more commonly used in radiocarbon dating and research applications and is not a typical choice for cancer treatment.

Phosphorus-32 is a radioactive isotope of phosphorus. It can be used in the treatment of certain skin cancers, specifically cutaneous T-cell lymphoma (CTCL). P-32 is applied topically to the skin as a radioactive ointment or cream, where its beta radiation can target and treat the cancer cells on or near the skin's surface.

Carbon-12 is the most abundant and stable isotope of carbon. It is not used in the treatment of cancer or for any medical applications. In the context of skin cancer, the most relevant option from the ones provided is c) Phosphorus-32 (P-32). P-32 is used for specific skin cancer treatments that target the surface of the skin, and its beta radiation can be effective in treating skin conditions like CTCL. It's important to note that the choice of treatment for skin cancer should be made by medical professionals based on the type and stage of cancer, as well as the patient's individual needs and medical history.

questions.explanation: For skin cancer phosphorous-32 or strontium-90 may be used. Phosphorus-32 is a radioactive isotope of phosphorus. It can be used in the treatment of certain skin cancers, specifically cutaneous T-cell lymphoma (CTCL). P-32 is applied topically to the skin as a radioactive ointment or cream, where its beta radiation can target and treat the cancer cells on or near the skin's surface.

questions.text: Radio-isotope Cobalt which emits high-energy γ-rays is used for the treatment of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Cobalt-60 is not used for the treatment of fever. Fever is typically a symptom of an underlying medical condition and is treated with medications or other therapies aimed at addressing the root cause of the fever.

Cobalt-60 is not used for the treatment of liver conditions directly. The liver may be affected by cancer, and in some cases, radiation therapy with high-energy gamma rays (from sources like Co-60) can be used to treat liver cancer. However, Co-60 is not a specific treatment for the liver itself.

Cobalt-60 is not commonly used for the treatment of bones, although radiation therapy is sometimes used to treat bone-related conditions, such as bone metastases from cancer. In such cases, high-energy gamma radiation may be employed, but it is not specific to Coba-60.

Cobalt-60 is primarily used in radiation therapy for the treatment of cancer. High-energy gamma rays emitted by Co-60 can be precisely directed to cancerous tissues to destroy or damage cancer cells, helping to treat various types of cancer, including those in the brain, head and neck, and other parts of the body. In summary, Cobalt-60 is primarily used for the treatment of cancer through radiation therapy, making option ''D " the correct choice.

questions.explanation: Cobalt-60 is primarily used in radiation therapy for the treatment of cancer. High-energy gamma rays emitted by Co-60 can be precisely directed to cancerous tissues to destroy or damage cancer cells, helping to treat various types of cancer, including those in the brain, head and neck, and other parts of the body. In summary, Cobalt-60 is primarily used for the treatment of cancer through radiation therapy, making option ''D'' the correct choice.

questions.text: Cancerous tissue in a thyroid gland can be detected by the intake of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. Radioactive Iodine, often in the form of Iodine-131 (I-131), is commonly used in the diagnosis and treatment of thyroid conditions, including thyroid cancer. The thyroid gland naturally takes up Iodine to produce thyroid hormones. Radioactive Iodine can be administered to a patient, and the thyroid gland preferentially absorbs it. By detecting the radioactive emissions from the iodine, such as gamma rays, medical professionals can identify thyroid tissue abnormalities, including cancerous growths.
Radioactive Carbon is not typically used for thyroid imaging or detection of thyroid cancer. Carbon-14 (C-14) is used for other applications, such as radiocarbon dating and research.
Radioactive Sodium is not commonly used for thyroid imaging or cancer detection. It may have other applications in medical research, but it is not a standard method for thyroid diagnostics.

Radioactive Phosphorous, such as phosphorus-32 (P-32), is not commonly used for thyroid imaging or cancer detection. P-32 is sometimes used in the treatment of skin conditions, but it is not typically associated with thyroid diagnosis or treatment.

questions.explanation: Radio-iodine is absorbed mostly by the thyroid gland. Radioactive iodine, often in the form of Iodine-131 (I-131), is commonly used in the diagnosis and treatment of thyroid conditions, including thyroid cancer. The thyroid gland naturally takes up iodine to produce thyroid hormones. Radioactive iodine can be administered to a patient, and the thyroid gland preferentially absorbs it. By detecting the radioactive emissions from the iodine, such as gamma rays, medical professionals can identify thyroid tissue abnormalities, including cancerous growths.
hints: Cancerous tissue in a thyroid gland

questions.text: Circulation of blood can be studied by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Sodium-24 is commonly used for the study of blood circulation. Sodium-24 is used primarily in research and medical applications related to sodium metabolism in the body. It is a standard tracer for studying blood circulation.

Carbon-14 is not typically used to study blood circulation directly. C-14 is commonly used in radiocarbon dating and various biological and biochemical studies. While it can be used to investigate biological processes, it is not a specific blood circulation tracer.

Strontium-90 is not used for the study of blood circulation. It is a radioactive isotope of Strontium that is primarily associated with nuclear fallout and environmental contamination. It is not employed for medical diagnostics or blood circulation studies.

Iodine-131 is sometimes used to study blood circulation, particularly for thyroid imaging and treatments. I-131 is taken up by the thyroid gland, and its circulation in the thyroid can be tracked for diagnostic purposes. While it is not a direct tracer for general blood circulation, it is relevant for specific thyroid-related studies.

questions.explanation: Sodium-24 is commonly used for the study of blood circulation. Sodium-24 is used primarily in research and medical applications related to sodium metabolism in the body. It is a standard tracer for studying blood circulation.

questions.text: Which one of the following is the most useful tracer?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Strontium-90 is a radioactive isotope of Strontium and is primarily associated with environmental contamination and nuclear fallout. It is not commonly used as a tracer for medical or diagnostic purposes. Strontium-90 is not a typical choice for medical imaging or diagnostics.

Carbon-14 is a widely used and versatile radioactive tracer in various scientific fields. It is commonly used in radiocarbon dating, as well as in biological and biochemical studies. C-14 can be used to trace the movement and behavior of carbon-containing compounds in living organisms. It is valuable for dating archaeological and geological samples.

Iodine-131 is commonly used in nuclear medicine, particularly for thyroid imaging and treatment. It is taken up by the thyroid gland and can be used to diagnose and treat thyroid conditions. I-131 is highly valuable for studying thyroid function and identifying thyroid-related disorders.

Cobalt-60 is primarily used in radiation therapy for the treatment of cancer. It emits high-energy gamma rays and is valuable for precisely targeting and destroying cancerous cells in various parts of the body. Co-60 is essential in oncology for radiation treatment planning and delivery.

questions.explanation: Carbon-14 is a widely used and versatile radioactive tracer in various scientific fields. It is commonly used in radiocarbon dating, as well as in biological and biochemical studies. C-14 can be used to trace the movement and behavior of carbon-containing compounds in living organisms. It is valuable for dating archaeological and geological samples.

questions.text: The isotopes of an element:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This statement is not accurate. Isotopes of an element can indeed be separated using various methods, including physical and chemical processes. For example, isotope separation techniques such as centrifugation, diffusion, or electromagnetic separation can be employed to separate isotopes.

This is generally true. Isotopes of the same element have the same number of protons in their nuclei, which determines their chemical behavior. The differences between isotopes primarily lie in their atomic mass, but their chemical properties are largely identical.

This statement is not accurate. Isotopes of an element are typically found together in nature, not well separated. Natural samples of an element consist of a mixture of its various isotopes. Separating them often requires specialized techniques.

options.text: Cannot be separated by physical method
options.explanation: This statement is not accurate. Isotopes can be separated by physical methods, as mentioned earlier. These methods include processes like distillation, filtration, and various forms of isotope separation, depending on the specific isotopes involved and the intended purpose.

questions.explanation: Chemical properties of all the isotopes of an element are similar, because the chemical properties of an element depend upon the number of electrons around the nucleus. Isotopes of an element have same number of electrons and same number of protons.

questions.text: An alpha particle is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: A helium atom with two electrons removed
options.explanation: An alpha particle is basically produced when a large unstable nucleus converts into a stable nucleus. This process also releases an alpha particle/ helium.

options.text: An aggregate of two or more electrons
options.explanation: Alpha particles are not aggregates of electrons. They are much larger and more massive than electrons. Electrons are negatively charged, whereas alpha particles are positively charged.

An alpha particle is not a hydrogen atom. A hydrogen atom consists of one proton and one electron. An alpha particle contains two protons and two neutrons, making it much heavier and positively charged.

options.text: The ultimate unit of positive charge
options.explanation: While alpha particles are positively charged, they are not the ultimate unit of positive charge. The concept of an "ultimate unit of positive charge" is not typically associated with alpha particles. Protons are often considered as fundamental particles carrying positive charge, and an alpha particle contains two protons.

questions.explanation: α-particles are helium nuclei (atom with two electrons removed). The charge on them is +2e while their mass is 4u that is every α-particle has two protons and two neutrons.

questions.text: A beta particle is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A beta particle is not a Helium nucleus (alpha particle). Beta particles are different from alpha particles. Beta particles can be either electrons (beta-minus, denoted as β-) or positrons (beta-plus, denoted as β+).
Beta particles are not elements themselves but rather subatomic particles. They are associated with certain types of radioactive decay, but they are not elements.

This is the correct description. Beta particles can be either electrons (beta-minus, β-) or positrons (beta-plus, β+), which are antiparticles of electrons. In beta-minus decay, an unstable nucleus emits an electron, while in beta-plus decay, it emits a positron.

Beta particles can be negatively charged, but they are specifically electrons or positrons. Other negative particles, such as muons or tauons, are different particles with distinct properties.In summary, a beta particle is either an electron (beta-minus) or a positron (beta-plus), and it is associated with certain types of radioactive decay processes involving the emission of these particles from unstable atomic nuclei.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct because β particles are the fast moving electrons or positrons which come out of the nucleus of a radioactive element.

Incorrect option. Isotones are species or atoms having the same number of neutrons.
Incorrect option. Isomers are compounds that have the same molecular formulae but different physical and chemical properties. (n-butane) (Iso-butane).

questions.explanation: Isotopes: Atoms of the same element with the same number of atomic numbers but a different mass number.Isotones: Species or atoms having the same number of neutrons.Isobars: Atoms having the same mass number but different atomic numbers. Isomers: Compounds have the same molecular formulae but different physical and chemical properties. (n-butane) (Iso-butane)

questions.text: Radioactive materials can be identified by measuring their:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The term "hardness" is typically associated with materials' resistance to deformation or scratching. It is not a property commonly used to identify radioactive materials. Radioactive materials are usually identified based on their radioactivity, not their hardness.

Mass, while important for characterizing materials, is not a specific property that is used to identify radioactive materials. The mass of a radioactive substance is determined by the sum of the masses of its constituent atoms and nuclei.

Density is a measure of mass per unit volume and can vary depending on the specific material. While density may provide some information about the material's composition, it is not a direct indicator of radioactivity. Radioactive materials are identified based on their radioactivity levels, not their densities.

This is the most accurate option for identifying and characterizing radioactive materials. The half-life of a radioactive substance is a specific property that indicates the time it takes for half of the radioactive atoms in a sample to decay. Different radioactive materials have different half-lives, which can be used to identify and differentiate them. Measuring the half-life of a radioactive material is a key method for identifying and studying radioisotopes.

questions.explanation: This is the most accurate option for identifying and characterizing radioactive materials. The half-life of a radioactive substance is a specific property that indicates the time it takes for half of the radioactive atoms in a sample to decay. Different radioactive materials have different half-lives, which can be used to identify and differentiate them. Measuring the half-life of a radioactive material is a key method for identifying and studying radioisotopes.

questions.text: The rate of decay of a radioactive substances:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is not accurate. The rate of decay of a radioactive substance is not constant with time. It is typically characterized by its half-life, which is the time it takes for half of the radioactive atoms in a sample to decay. As time passes, the rate of decay decreases because there are fewer radioactive atoms remaining to decay.

This is generally true. As mentioned earlier, the rate of decay decreases with time. Initially, the activity is high, but it gradually decreases as the radioactive atoms decay. The rate follows an exponential decay curve.
This is not accurate. The rate of decay does not increase with time. It consistently decreases as the radioactive material decays.

options.text: May increase or decrease with time
options.explanation: This statement is not accurate in the context of typical radioactive decay. The rate of decay of a specific radioactive substance follows a well-defined pattern of exponential decay, which results in a consistent decrease in activity with time. While there can be variations in decay rates for some highly unstable isotopes or in specific nuclear reactions, these are not common and typically follow specific conditions, such as in certain nuclear reactions or extreme environments.

questions.explanation: Rate of decay of radioactive substance decreases exponentially with time.

questions.text: The half-life of radioactive element depends on:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The half-life of a radioactive element is generally not significantly affected by temperature. Radioactive decay is a nuclear process that occurs independently of temperature variations. The energy levels involved in nuclear reactions are much higher than the thermal energies, so changes in temperature do not have a substantial impact on the rate of decay.

The half-life of a radioactive element is primarily determined by the nature of the element itself. Each radioactive isotope has a specific and unique half-life, which is characteristic of that isotope. It is an inherent property of the radioactive element and is not influenced by other factors.

Like temperature, pressure does not have a significant effect on the half-life of a radioactive element. Radioactive decay is a nuclear process, and changes in pressure do not alter the rate of nuclear reactions significantly. The pressure is typically not a relevant factor in determining half-life.

The half-life of a radioactive element is independent of the amount of the radioactive substance. Whether you have a small sample or a large sample of a radioactive element, the half-life remains the same. The half-life is a constant characteristic of the radioactive isotope itself and is not influenced by the quantity of the substance.

questions.explanation: The half-life of a radioactive element is primarily determined by the nature of the element itself. Each radioactive isotope has a specific and unique half-life, which is characteristic of that isotope. It is an inherent property of the radioactive element and is not influenced by other factors.T1/2λ = In2T1/2 =In2/λ

questions.text: The half-life of a radioactive substance is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Half the time it takes for the entire substance to decay
options.explanation: This is an inaccurate description of the half-life. The half-life is the time it takes for half of a sample of a radioactive substance to decay. It's a measure of the rate of radioactive decay.

This is not a general statement. The half-life of a radioactive substance can vary widely, and it is not typically around 50 years. Half-lives can range from fractions of a second to millions of years, depending on the specific radioactive isotope.

options.text: The time for Radium to change into Lead
options.explanation: This option does not describe the concept of half-life accurately. The process of one radioactive element changing into another through radioactive decay is not directly related to the concept of half-life.

options.text: The time for half the substance to decay
options.explanation: This is the most accurate description of half-life. The half-life is the time it takes for half of the radioactive atoms in a sample to decay. It is a fundamental parameter used to characterize the stability and rate of decay of a radioactive substance.

questions.explanation: The half-life "T1/2" of a radioactive element is that period in which half of the atoms decay. This is the most accurate description of half-life. The half-life is the time it takes for half of the radioactive atoms in a sample to decay. It is a fundamental parameter used to characterize the stability and rate of decay of a radioactive substance.

questions.text: The particle which has its mass number and charge number equal to zero:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A proton has a mass number of 1 (since it is found in the nucleus) and a charge number of +1. Therefore, it does not have a mass number and charge number equal to zero.
A neutron has a mass number of 1 (found in the nucleus) and a charge number of 0 (neutral). It does not have a mass number and charge number equal to zero.
An electron has a mass number of 0 (it is much lighter than protons and neutrons) and a charge number of -1. It does not have a mass number and charge number equal to zero.
A photon is a fundamental particle of light and electromagnetic radiation. It has no rest mass (mass number is zero), and it carries no electric charge (charge number is zero).

questions.explanation: Photon is a chargeless and massless particle. A photon is a fundamental particle of light and electromagnetic radiation. It has no rest mass (mass number is zero), and it carries no electric charge (charge number is zero).

questions.text: Marie Curie and Pierre Curie discovered two new radioactive elements which are:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Uranium was already known as a radioactive element and was discovered long before the Curies' work. Polonium, on the other hand, was one of the elements discovered by the Curies.
While Uranium was indeed a known radioactive element, Radium was one of the elements discovered by the Curies. This option is partially correct.

This is the correct choice. Marie Curie discovered Polonium in 1898, and shortly after, she and Pierre Curie discovered Radium in the same year. These discoveries were significant contributions to the field of radioactivity.
Uranium was known prior to the Curies' work, and Plutonium is a synthetic element that was not discovered until the 20th century, long after the Curies' discoveries.

questions.explanation: Marie Curie and Pierre Curie discovered two new radioactive elements that they called Polonium and Radium.

questions.text: Which one of the following possesses maximum velocity?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Alpha rays (α): Alpha particles are composed of two protons and two neutrons, which makes them relatively massive and positively charged. Due to their larger mass and charge, they have relatively low velocities. They typically move at speeds of around 5% the speed of light.

Gamma rays (γ): Gamma rays are electromagnetic radiation, similar to X-rays, and they are composed of high-energy photons. They move at the speed of light (approximately 299,792,458 meters per second in a vacuum), which is the maximum velocity possible for anything in the universe.

Beta rays (β): Beta particles are high-energy electrons (beta-minus) or positrons (beta-plus). They have higher velocities compared to alpha particles, but they are still slower than gamma rays. Beta particles can move at various speeds depending on their kinetic energy, which can vary from a few percent to nearly the speed of light.

options.text: All of the above have same speed
options.explanation: Among the options given, gamma rays have the maximum velocity because they travel at the speed of light, which is faster than both alpha and beta particles.

questions.explanation: Gamma rays have the maximum velocity because they travel at the speed of light, which is faster than both alpha and beta particles.

questions.text: Which of the following will be a better shield against 𝛾-rays?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. While water can attenuate gamma rays, a very large thickness is required to provide effective shielding.
This option is incorrect. Although heavy water has a slightly higher density than ordinary water, it is still not as effective as lead in shielding gamma rays.
This option is correct. Lead is an excellent shield against gamma rays due to its high density and atomic number, which effectively absorb the radiation.
This option is incorrect. Aluminum has a lower density compared to Lead, making it less effective in shielding against gamma rays.

questions.explanation: Gamma rays are known to have maximum penetration they can penetrate several metals. The best shielding against them is elements with high atomic numbers and high density, such as lead. The atomic number of Lead is 82.Gamma rays travel at the speed of light, and they can travel thousands of meters in the air before spending their energy. They can be shielded by water but a huge amount would be required: 13.8 feet of water, about 6.6 feet of concrete, or about 1.3 feet of Lead is required to shield against gamma rays. Aluminium is less dense and gamma rays can penetrate through them completely.

questions.text: The ionizing power when we compare α, β and γ rays is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is not accurate. The ionizing power of α, β, and γ radiation is not the same. They have different ionization abilities.

This is the correct option. Among α, β, and γ radiation, α-particles have the highest ionizing power. This is because α-particles are relatively massive and carry a double positive charge (two protons), making them highly ionizing as they interact with electrons and other atoms in their path.
β-particles are less massive than α-particles and carry a single charge, so their ionizing power is lower than that of α-particles but higher than that of γ-rays.

γ-rays are electromagnetic waves (high-energy photons) and do not carry an electric charge. They have the lowest ionizing power among α, β, and γ radiation. While they can ionize atoms through indirect processes, their ionization power is much lower than that of α- and β-particles.

questions.explanation: Ionization ability in increasing order is given byγ<β<α

questions.text: What type of sentence is this?Our car broke down, so we came last.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: This not a complex sentence. A complex sentence has one independent clause and at least one dependent clause.
This not a simple sentence. A simple sentence has only one independent clause.

This sentence is an example of a compound sentence.It contains two independent clauses (main clauses):"Our car broke down" and "we came last"These clauses are joined by the coordinating conjunction "so", which indicates a cause-and-effect relationship between the two clauses. Both clauses could stand alone as separate sentences, but they are combined to show the relationship between the car breaking down and the consequence of arriving late.
This not a compound-complex sentence. A compound-complex sentence has two or more independent clauses and at least one dependent clause.

questions.explanation: This sentence is an example of a compound sentence.It contains two independent clauses (main clauses):"Our car broke down" and "we came last"These clauses are joined by the coordinating conjunction "so", which indicates a cause-and-effect relationship between the two clauses. Both clauses could stand alone as separate sentences, but they are combined to show the relationship between the car breaking down and the consequence of arriving late.

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because it is not the correct pair of words to be used here."Write, hoping" This option is grammatically incorrect in this context.

This option is incorrect because it is not the correct pair of words to be used here."Am writing, hope" correctly uses the present continuous tense ("am writing") to describe an action in progress and the simple present tense ("hope") to express a general feeling or expectation.

This option is incorrect because it is not the correct pair of words to be used here."Wrote, am hopeful" This option uses the past tense ("wrote") which doesn't fit the ongoing nature of the action, and "am hopeful" doesn't work as well as "hope" in this context.
This option is incorrect because it is not the correct pair of words to be used here.

questions.explanation: Option B is the correct pair of words to be used here. because it correctly uses the present continuous tense ("am writing") to describe an action in progress and the simple present tense ("hope")

questions.text: Gold is _ precious metal.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Here "a" is used as an indefinite article and indefinite article is used before a singular countable noun. In this sentence, precious metal is a singular countable noun.
This option is incorrect. The article "an" is used before words that begin with a vowel sound. However, "precious" does not begin with a vowel sound.

This option is incorrect. "The" is not used in this sentence because "gold" is referred to in a general sense. When using "the," it implies that the listener or reader understands which specific gold is being discussed. For example, "The gold found in the river was mistaken for a precious metal."
This option is also incorrect. While it may be tempting to think that When an article is needed, it is common to use an article when referring to a general category or class of something.

questions.explanation: In the phrase "Gold is a precious metal," the article "a" is used because it precedes the noun "precious metal" to indicate that gold belongs to a category or class of substances known as precious metals. "A" is an indefinite article used before singular nouns to refer to any member of a general category. In this case, "precious metal" refers to a broad category of metals that are rare, valuable, and often used in jewelry or currency.

questions.text: Which one of the following has four sides?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: A triangle has 3 sides as “tri” is in the name which means 3.
A square has 4 corners hence it has 4 sides.
A circle has no sides as a side requires two distinct end-points joined by a straight line. For a circle, this will never be the case.
A triangle has 3 sides as “tri” is in the name which means 3. A right-angled triangle also has 3 sides.

questions.explanation: A triangle has 3 sides as “tri” is in the name which means 3. A right-angled triangle also has 3 sides. A circle has no sides as a side requires two distinct end-points joined by a straight line. For a circle, this will never be the case. A square has 4 corners hence it has 4 sides.
